L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the application does not enforce a minimum length or entropy requirement on the signing secret used for token-based authentication. Secrets as short as one byte are silently accepted, making them trivially brute-forceable offline from a single captured token, allowing an attacker to forge valid tokens for any user including administrators.

L3.area: JWT secret configuration parsing and validation logic
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the HS256 JWT signing secret is decoded from a base64-encoded configuration value but no minimum key length is enforced. Secrets shorter than the 32-byte minimum required by RFC 7518 for HMAC-SHA256 are silently accepted, enabling offline brute-force recovery of the signing key from a single captured JWT and subsequent token forgery for arbitrary users.

ground_truth.reason: The JWTSecret configuration field accepts any base64-decodable value without enforcing a minimum length, allowing HS256 signing keys shorter than 32 bytes that are trivially brute-forceable, enabling full account takeover via token forgery.
